name: US Real GDP per Capita (MeasuringWorth)

book_quotes.quote: We begin with the long view. Figures 2.1–2.3 depict the paths of US industrial production, real investment, and real GNP per capita, respectively, over periods of about 150 years.

Figure 2.3 US Real GDP per Capita, 1889–2010. 1790–2010, from Measuring Worth.com at http://measuringworth.com/usgdp/. Their sources and methods are described in their link “Source and Techniques Used in the Construction of Annual GDP, 1790 – Present.”

primary_source.publication: MeasuringWorth, “Annual GDP of the United States, 1790–Present”
primary_source.units: Real GDP per capita, constant dollars (per MeasuringWorth methodology)
primary_source.license: MeasuringWorth terms (free for academic use with attribution)

extension_candidates.units: Real GDP per capita, constant dollars
concerns: Same source as original; direct continuation with re-indexing only if dollar base year changes.

methodology_notes: Direct port of MeasuringWorth's real GDP per capita series per Appendix 2.1 (p. 763).
Note caption discrepancy: Appendix 2.1 says 1790–2010 but Fig 2.3 plots 1889–2010 (year_range_book reflects plotted range).
MeasuringWorth synthesizes Kuznets, Balke-Gordon, and BEA NIPA estimates.
open_questions: MeasuringWorth occasionally revises historical estimates; document the access date when extending.
